# Tags Input

Collects multiple short values as keyboard-navigable, removable tags.

## Installation

Add Tags Input from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/tags-input
```

## Usage

Use Tags Input for a small set of user-created values such as keywords, recipients, or skills. Bind `v-model` to an array, render one `TagsInputItem` per value, and place `TagsInputInput` after the items.

**Technology tags example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="grid w-full max-w-md gap-2">
    <Label for="technologies">Technologies</Label>
    <TagsInput
      id="technologies"
      v-model="tags"
    >
      <TagsInputItem v-for="tag in tags" :key="tag" :value="tag">
        <TagsInputItemText />
        <TagsInputItemDelete />
      </TagsInputItem>
      <TagsInputInput placeholder="Add a technology…" />
    </TagsInput>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref } from 'vue'
import { Label } from '@/Components/Label'
import {
  TagsInput,
  TagsInputInput,
  TagsInputItem,
  TagsInputItemDelete,
  TagsInputItemText,
} from '@/Components/TagsInput'

const tags = ref(['Vue', 'Laravel'])
</script>
```

## Creating tags

Enter and the configured `delimiter` add the current text. Comma is the default delimiter. Enable `add-on-paste` to split pasted text by that delimiter, `add-on-tab` to commit before focus moves, or `add-on-blur` to commit when the field loses focus. Enable these shortcuts only when users will understand that unfinished text becomes a value.

## Duplicates, limits, and events

Duplicate tags are rejected by default. Set `duplicate` only when repeated values have a real meaning, and set `max` to cap the number of tags. The root emits `add-tag`, `remove-tag`, and `invalid`; use `invalid` to explain a duplicate or limit failure without discarding the existing model.

## Object values

String arrays are simplest for free-form tags. Object values require `convert-value` to turn typed text into the model type and `display-value` to render a readable label. Prefer stable ids with a searchable selection component when users choose from a large canonical dataset rather than inventing new values.

## Keyboard and accessibility

Give the root an `id` and pair it with a visible label; that id is applied to the internal text input. At an empty input, Backspace selects the last tag and a second deletion removes it. Arrow keys, Home, and End move through tags. Keep the provided item text and delete primitives so selection and removal remain perceivable, and do not encode essential meaning by color alone.

## Forms and validation

Set `name` and `required` on the root for form participation. Validate each item and the complete array in surrounding form state—for example, allowed character sets, length, and maximum count. Use `disabled` for the entire control or disable only an item that must not be removed.

## Choosing a multi-value control

> **Use Combobox for a searchable option set: [Combobox](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/combobox)**
>
> When values must come from an existing list, use a multiple Combobox rather than accepting arbitrary tags.

> **Use Input for one short value: [Input](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/input)**
>
> If only one value is accepted, a labeled Input is simpler and avoids tag creation and removal behavior.

Hosted documentation: [Tags Input](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/tags-input)
